title: Graph Neural Network Explanations are Fragile
abstract: Explainable Graph Neural Network (GNN) has emerged recently to foster the trust of using GNNs. Existing GNN explainers are developed from various perspectives to enhance the explanation performance. We take the first step to study GNN explainers under adversarial attack-We found that an adversary slightly perturbing graph structure can ensure GNN model makes correct predictions, but the GNN explainer yields a drastically different explanation on the perturbed graph. Specifically, we first formulate the attack problem under a practical threat model (i.e., the adversary has limited knowledge about the GNN explainer and a restricted perturbation budget). We then design two methods (i.e., one is loss-based and the other is deduction-based) to realize the attack. We evaluate our attacks on various GNN explainers and the results show these explainers are fragile.1
impact: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) become the mainstream methodology for learning graph data. Explainable GNNs aim to foster the trust of using GNNs. However, this paper shows that state-of-the-art GNN explainers can be broken by practical, stealthy, and faithful adversarial attacks. Such vulnerabilities raise the concerns of using GNNs for safety/security-critical applications such as disease diagnosis and malware detection. Our findings urge the community to design more (provably) robust GNN explainers.

query_input.scenario: Automated fairness compliance auditing of GNN models
query_input.issue: Node injection attacks distort fairness explanations, hiding discrimination
hazard: Post-hoc GNN explainers are vulnerable to graph perturbations that alter explanations without changing predictions.
exposure: Auditors and regulators using GNN explainers to detect unfair model decisions

hipporag_result.vulnerability: Auditors lack robustness checks on GNN explainers, leaving them unable to detect when node injection attacks fake fairness explanations.
hipporag_result.impact: Discriminatory models pass compliance unseen, leading to biased automated decisions that harm protected groups.
hipporag_result.dose_response: Post-hoc GNN explainers' susceptibility to prediction-preserving perturbations, through auditors' reliance for fairness checks, triggers undetected node injection attacks that fake explanations, leading to discriminatory models passing compliance and harming protected groups.

dose_response: Post-hoc GNN explainer vulnerability to node injection attacks enables altered fairness explanations that, when auditors trust them without robustness testing, lead to biased models being certified as fair, perpetuating discrimination.
vulnerability: Auditors trust GNN fairness metrics without adversarial robustness testing, missing manipulated explanations.
impact: Biased models are certified as fair, perpetuating discrimination in credit, hiring, or justice.
